file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 心脏康复后的持续运动建议：通过日常活动（轻度家务和园艺）及医生批准的运动（步行、游泳、骑自行车）保持锻炼；运动注意事项——适时休息、胸痛或呼吸困难时停止、避免极端温度。
text_en: After completing the cardiac rehab program, continue with regular exercise through daily activities such as light yard and housework. Other good exercise options with your doctor's approval are walking, swimming, and bicycling. As you exercise, keep these precautions in mind. Rest as needed. Stop if you feel chest pain or have shortness of breath. And don't exercise when temperatures are very hot or very cold.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 压力与心理健康管理：压力影响血压和健康生活方式的维持，需识别和管理压力源；心梗后可能出现抑郁，可通过药物、心理咨询或互助小组获得帮助。
text_en: Stress can affect your blood pressure and your ability to maintain a healthy lifestyle. Identifying the causes of your stress and learning to eliminate or manage them can help with your recovery. You may feel depressed. Just these feelings with your doctor. He or she can prescribe medicine, counseling, or a support group to help you adjust.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 术后伤口监测与心绞痛症状识别：每日检查手术切口有无感染迹象（发热、红肿、疼痛、脓液、出血）；警惕胸部疼痛、压迫感、紧缩感等症状，这些可能提示心血管阻塞（心绞痛）。
text_en: It is important to stay aware of changes in your symptoms and know when to get help. If your hospital stay included a procedure with an incision, check the site each day for any signs of infection. Call your doctor if you develop a fever or redness, heat, pain, tenderness, pus, or bleeding at the incision site. Be mindful of any symptoms of chest pain, pressure, tightness, heaviness, ache, or any other kind of unusual sensation in your chest as any of these could mean you have a blockage in the blood vessels of the heart. Symptoms related to blockage in the blood vessels of the heart are called angina and could be any one of these symptoms.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 心绞痛的放射部位与紧急处理：心绞痛可放射至手臂、肩部、颈部、下颌、喉咙或背部；出现心绞痛应联系医生；若药物不能缓解心绞痛、伴呼吸困难、冷汗或头晕，需立即拨打急救电话。
text_en: Angina symptoms may also be felt in your arms, shoulders, neck, jaw, throat, or back. Call your doctor if you think you're experiencing angina. Call 911 immediately if you have any of these symptoms. Angina that doesn't go away with medication is prescribed by your doctor. Shortness of breath. Cold sweats. Or lightheadedness.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 介绍抗凝药物（血液稀释剂）的用途：自行注射抗凝剂，帮助预防血管内血栓形成
text_en: This video will teach you how to give yourself a shot of anti-coagulant medicine, also called a blood thinner. Blood thinners help prevent clots from forming inside your blood vessels.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 注射前准备物品：酒精棉片、预充式抗凝剂注射器、利器盒；介绍注射器主要部件（药筒、推杆、针帽、针头）
text_en: Before you begin, you will need alcohol wipes, your pre-filled syringe of anti-coagulant medicine, and a sharp spot. The main parts of the syringe are the barrel containing the medicine, the plunger, the needle cap, and the needle.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 选择注射部位：肚脐左右两侧至少两英寸处或大腿；护理人员也可在背部、上臂或上臀部注射；每天更换注射部位以避免肿胀或瘀伤
text_en: Select your injection site at least two inches to the right or left of your belly button or on either of your thighs. A caregiver may also use these instructions to give you a shot on either side of your back, upper arms, or upper buttocks. Use a different injection site each day to avoid swelling or bruising.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 用药注意事项：每天同一时间注射，遵医嘱，不跳过剂量，不超量注射
text_en: And take your shot at the same time each day. Be sure to follow your doctor's instructions. Do not skip any doses. Do not inject more medicine than prescribed.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 步骤1-2：用肥皂和水洗手并擦干；用酒精棉片以画圈方式清洁注射部位皮肤
text_en: Step 1. Before you begin, wash your hands with soap and water and then dry them. Step 2. Clean the skin over the injection site by rubbing with an alcohol wipe in a circular motion.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 步骤3-4：撕开铝箔封盖取出注射器（不可推穿封盖）；侧握药筒直拔针帽，不要按压推杆排出气泡
text_en: Step 3. To open your syringe, peel the foil cover off the back. Do not push the syringe through the foil cover because it could damage the syringe. Step 4. Hold the syringe sideways by the barrel. Then, pull the needle cap straight off without bending the needle. There will be an air bubble in the medicine. Do not press the plunger to squirt the air bubble out of the barrel.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 步骤5-6：捏起注射部位约一英寸皮肤，以90度角将针头刺入皮下脂肪组织；缓慢推注全部药物（包括气泡）
text_en: Step 5. With your free hand, pinch and hold an inch of skin at the injection site. Insert the needle at a 90-degree angle through your skin and into the fatty tissue beneath it. Step 6. Slowly press the plunger to deliver all of the medicine, including the air bubble.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 步骤7-9：垂直拔出针头；用力按下推杆激活安全护针装置；将用过的注射器丢入利器盒
text_en: Step 7. Pull the needle straight out of your skin. Step 8. Press down firmly on the plunger to activate the safety guard which covers the needle. Step 9. Throw away the used syringe in your sharp spots.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的定义：也称充血性心力衰竭（CHF），是一种终身性疾病，心脏仍在工作但泵血功能不足，无法为身体提供足够的血液和氧气
text_en: Heart failure, also called congestive heart failure, or CHF, is a lifelong condition in which your heart continues to work, but not as well as it should. Your heart can't pump enough blood and oxygen to meet the needs of your body.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的症状：液体在肺部、腿部和其他组织中积聚，导致呼吸困难、慢性咳嗽或喘息、腿脚或腹部肿胀、体重增加、持续疲劳、恶心或食欲不振、心悸、难以平躺呼吸等。长期心衰会削弱心脏并缩短寿命
text_en: As fluid builds up in the lungs, legs, and other body tissues, the symptoms of heart failure include shortness of breath, chronic coughing or wheezing, swelling of the legs, feet or abdomen, weight gain, feeling tired all the time, nausea or lack of appetite, heart palpitations, difficulty lying flat due to feelings of suffocation, and having to sit up to breathe. Over time, heart failure weakens your heart and can shorten your life if not well-managed.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 管理心衰的总体策略：遵医嘱、采取有益心脏的生活方式、关注症状变化，遵从医疗团队指导以管理症状并减少住院
text_en: Fortunately, you can help control your symptoms by following your doctor's instructions, opting a heart-healthy lifestyle, and staying aware of changes in your symptoms. Following the instructions of your healthcare team is very important in managing your symptoms and preventing unnecessary trips to the hospital.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 药物治疗：严格按处方服药，不要停药或漏服，注意副作用。常用药物包括ACE抑制剂、ARB、ARNI、β受体阻滞剂、盐皮质激素受体拮抗剂（醛固酮阻滞剂）和SGLT2抑制剂。不要擅自服用非处方药或补充剂
text_en: This includes taking your medications exactly as prescribed by your doctor. Don't stop taking your medications or skip doses. Take note of any side effects and tell your doctor. If the cost of your medication is an issue, discuss other options with your doctor. Your medications may include angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitors, angiotensin-2 receptor blockers, or angiotensin receptor-neprolycin inhibitors, beta blockers. Mineralo corticoid receptor antagonists, also known as aldosterone blockers, or sodium glucose cotransporter-2 inhibitors. These medicines are important in managing heart failure. Don't take any over-the-counter medication or supplement without asking your doctor if

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 随访与疫苗：按时参加所有复诊和化验预约，按医嘱接种流感和肺炎疫苗
text_en: Make and keep all of your follow-up appointments for both doctor's visits and lab tests. Get vaccinated against the flu and pneumonia as directed by your doctor. Taking your heart failure also means adopting a healthy lifestyle.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 心脏健康饮食：低脂、低胆固醇、高纤维饮食，有助于维持良好血压和体重。限制每日钠摄入量在2000毫克以下，避免烹饪和餐桌加盐，远离加工食品和快餐，比较食品标签选择低钠食品
text_en: You may need to make the following changes. Eat a heart-healthy diet including low fat, localesterole, and high fiber foods. A heart-healthy diet helps you maintain or achieve a good blood pressure and weight and helps prevent other conditions that may further stress your heart. Check food labels for the amounts of these ingredients and others. Eat your salt and sodium intake to 2,000 milligrams a day, which is less than a teaspoon by doing the following. Don't add salt when you cook or to food at the table. Avoid processed and fast foods. Compare food labels and choose the items that are lower in salt and sodium. Limit your alcohol intake.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 限酒、限液、戒烟：限制酒精摄入，控制液体摄入以减轻心脏负担，戒烟——尼古丁会升高心率和血压，同时减少输送到身体的含氧血液
text_en: Ask your doctor if your condition allows you to include any alcohol in your diet. After your fluid intake, too much fluid makes your heart work harder. Ask your doctor how much fluid is the right amount for you. Don't smoke. Nicotine increases heart rate and blood pressure. At the same time, it decreases the amount of oxygen-rich blood delivered to your body's Become physically active.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 体力活动与心脏康复：通过日常活动和散步、游泳、骑车等运动保持活跃，注意运动时适当休息、出现胸痛或异常气短时停止、避免极端温度。医生可能还会处方心脏康复计划，包括医学监督下的运动、生活方式咨询和健康教育
text_en: Regular exercise through daily activities such as gardening and housework can make you feel better. Other good exercise options with your doctor's approval are walking, swimming and bicycling. As you exercise, keep these precautions in mind. Rest as needed. Stop if you feel chest pain or more than usual shortness of breath. And don't exercise when temperatures are very hot or very cold. Your doctor may also prescribe a cardiac rehabilitation program that is a combination of medically supervised exercise, lifestyle counseling and education.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 症状监测——绿灯区：用交通灯颜色评估症状状态。绿色表示症状受控，应继续遵医嘱、保持复诊。每天固定时间称重以监测体液潴留，记录体重变化
text_en: It is important to stay aware of changes in your symptoms and know when to get help. Using the traffic light colors, green, yellow and red to assess how you feel can help you take the proper action to manage your heart failure symptoms. Green is your goal and means that your symptoms are under control. Continue to follow your doctor's directions and keep all medical appointments. Wear yourself daily to see if your body is retaining fluids. Do it at the same time each day before eating or drinking and wear the same type of clothing or no clothes at all. Record and take note of any changes in your weight.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 症状监测——黄灯区（警告信号）：一天内体重增加2-3磅或一周增加5磅、呼吸困难加重、因呼吸困难难以入睡、持续快速或不规则心跳、头晕或晕厥感，应立即联系医生
text_en: Symptoms in the yellow zone are warning signs. Call your doctor immediately if you gained 2-3 or more pounds in one day or 5 pounds in Have an increased shortness of breath. Have difficulty sleeping due to trouble breathing. Will persistent, fast or irregular heartbeats are feeling light-headed or as if you may faint.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 症状监测——红灯区（紧急情况）：胸痛持续超过15分钟、严重且持续的呼吸困难、咳出粉红色泡沫痰或晕厥，应立即拨打急救电话911
text_en: Red zone symptoms are emergencies that mean you need medical evaluation right away. Call 911 if you experience chest pain that lasts more than 15 minutes. Severe don going shortness of breath. Coughing up pink frothy foam or fainting.

title: ANH00007 General Anesthesia
summary_zh: 术前静脉通路建立：通过套管针在手臂静脉建立IV通路，用于输注液体和药物直接进入血液，并可给予镇静药物帮助患者放松。
text_en: Before your procedure, an IV line will be placed in a vein in your arm using a small tube called a cannula. The IV will deliver fluid and medications directly into your bloodstream. You may receive some medication to help you relax.

title: ANH00007 General Anesthesia
summary_zh: 术前监护设备安置：患者被安置在手术台上，依次放置血压袖带监测血压、胸部电极片监测心率、指夹式脉搏血氧仪监测血氧饱和度，麻醉医师通过这些设备在术前、术中和术后持续监测生命体征。
text_en: You will be placed on the operating table and made as comfortable as possible. A blood pressure cuff will be placed on your arm to check your blood pressure readings. Sticky pads will be placed on your chest to check your heart rate. And a clip will be put on your finger to check your body's oxygen levels. These devices allow the anesthesia specialist to closely monitor your vital signs before, during and after your procedure.

title: ANH00007 General Anesthesia
summary_zh: 麻醉诱导与气道管理：通过面罩吸入麻醉气体或静脉注射药物使患者入睡，随后经口插入气管导管至气管内，导管连接呼吸机辅助呼吸并输送麻醉气体至肺部。
text_en: You will begin receiving general anesthesia by either breathing anesthetic gases through a mask or through IV injection, which will cause you to fall asleep. Once you are asleep, you will be given a mixture of oxygen and anesthetic gases either through your mask or through a special tube inserted through your mouth and into your windpipe. The tube is attached to a respirator, which helps you breathe while delivering the gases to your lungs.

title: ANH00007 General Anesthesia
summary_zh: 全身麻醉的作用机制：麻醉气体在肺组织深处被吸收进入血液，由血细胞携带至大脑，阻断大脑接收来自身体神经的信号，使患者在手术期间保持无意识、无痛觉，且醒后无手术记忆。
text_en: Deep in your lung tissues, the gases are absorbed into your bloodstream and carried by blood cells to your brain. The anesthesia prevents your brain from receiving messages from nerves in your body. As a result, you will remain asleep and pain-free during your procedure, and you will have no memory of it when you wake up.

title: ANH00007 General Anesthesia
summary_zh: 术后苏醒与恢复：麻醉医师给予拮抗药物逆转麻醉效果，患者快速苏醒后拔除气管导管；转入恢复室密切监护并按需给予镇痛药物；患者可能出现短暂头晕和轻微意识模糊，待生命体征稳定后转入病房或出院。
text_en: After your operation, the anesthesia specialist will give you medications to reverse the anesthesia, and you will awaken quickly. If you had a breathing tube in place, he or she will remove it as soon as you can safely breathe on your own. You will be taken to the recovery room where you will be closely monitored, and given pain medication is needed. You may feel light-headed and slightly disoriented, but this feeling should pass quickly. Once you are stable, you will be sent to a hospital room or home.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 引言：说明当心脏传导系统（控制心跳的电信号系统）无法正常工作时，外科医生会植入起搏器。
text_en: A surgeon will insert a pacemaker into your body if your heart's conduction system, the electrical system that makes your heart beat, is not working properly.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 心脏基本解剖结构：心脏是四腔肌性器官，血液先汇入上方两个心房，再充入下方两个心室；右心室将血液泵向肺部，左心室将血液泵向全身。
text_en: The heart is a muscular organ with four chambers. Blood collects in the heart's two upper chambers or atria before filling the two lower chambers or ventricles. In the ventricles contract, blood in the right ventricle is pumped to the lungs and blood in the left ventricle is pumped to the rest of the body.
